following information:
Ensure ComEd Has Access to Project Site

maintenance of electrical equipment, customers 
are responsible for the following:

The proper installation of a grounding system to 
an underground water piping system is as follows:

1) What is the difference between the City of Chicago and 
Outside of Chicago for New Business request?
2) Is conduit required in the rear lot?

3) Is conduit required for meters located in excess of 100ft 
from easement?
4) Can I be charged for illegal connections?

5) Can I install an overhead meter fitting that is feeding an 
underground service or vice versa?

6) Why do I have to relocate or upgrade my meter when installing 
solar or EV installation?

7) Can a second meter fitting be installed on an attached garage/
structure without a second service drop?
8) Can I temp out my own service since ComEd is taking too long?
9) Can I request 3-phase service for my residential home?
10) Why can't I install a ring style meter fitting?
11) What is the difference between rear lot and front lot?
12) When are meters to be installed outside?

13) At the weather head, should I label the neutral wire with white tape? 
Also should the line and load sides be marked?

14) If I am reducing the number of meters, why can't I just leave the 
empty fitting(s)? I am not using the blank meter fitting.
15) Where can I get more information about solar or electric vehicles?

When the lights 
go out on a stormy 
night, how long 
are you willing 
to wait?
